Class BadToolResponse

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.agi.event.BasicPropertyChangeSource
uno.anahata.asi.agi.tool.spi.AbstractToolResponse<BadToolCall>
uno.anahata.asi.agi.tool.spi.bad.BadToolResponse
All Implemented Interfaces:
PropertyChangeSource, Rebindable
public class BadToolResponse extends AbstractToolResponse<BadToolCall>
The response for a BadToolCall. Its status is immediately set to ToolExecutionStatus.DECLINED and its execute method is a no-op.
Author:
anahata-gemini-pro-2.5

    Method
    Description
    void
    addAttachment(byte[] data, String mimeType)
    Attaches a binary blob to this response.
    void
    addError(String error)
    Adds a log message to this response, which can be displayed to the user.
    void
    addError(Throwable t)
    Converts a exception to a string and adds it to the errors field.
    void
    addLog(String message)
    Adds a log message to this response, which can be displayed to the user.
    String
    asText()
    Returns the content of the response as a simple string.
    void
    clearLogs()
    Clears any log messages captured during execution.
    void
    decline()
    Resets the response to its initial state, clearing results, errors, logs, and attachments.
    void
    fail(String reason)
    Rejects the tool call before execution, setting the status to FAILED.
    void
    fail(String reason, String details)
    Rejects the tool call before execution with detailed context.
    Agi
    getAgi()
    Gets the parent agi session.
    List<ToolResponseAttachment>
    getAttachments()
    Gets an unmodifiable view of the attachments.
    Map<String,Object>
    getExecutedArgs()
    Gets the effective arguments that were used for execution.
    int
    getTokenCount()
    Retrieves the cached token count for this response.
    String
    getToolName()
    Gets the name of the tool that was invoked.
    void
    rebind()
    Re-initializes the transient PropertyChangeSupport after deserialization.
    void
    removeAttachment(ToolResponseAttachment attachment)
    Removes an attachment from this response.
    void
    resetTokenCount()
    Resets the cached token count, forcing a lazy, background recalculation on the next call to getTokenCount().
    void
    setErrors(String errors)
    Sets the error message and updates the token count.
    void
    setExpanded(boolean expanded)
    Sets the persistent UI state for the response expansion and fires a property change event.
    void
    setResult(Object result)
    Sets the result of the tool execution and updates the token count.
    void
    setStatus(ToolExecutionStatus status)
    Sets the status of the tool execution and fires a property change event.
    void
    setThread(Thread thread)
    Sets the running thread and updates the thread name.

      public BadToolResponse(@NonNull @NonNull BadToolCall call)

  • Method Details

    • execute

      public void execute()
      Description copied from class: AbstractToolResponse
      Executes the tool logic. This method is responsible for populating the status, result, errors, and executionTimeMillis fields.
      Specified by:
      execute in class AbstractToolResponse<BadToolCall>

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Description copied from class: AbstractToolResponse
      Stops the tool execution if it is currently running.
